title: Fix remote audio not audible during video calls

requirements.text: Fix the video call feature so that the remote party's audio is audible during a call. The VideoCallModal must correctly attach the remote media stream (including audio tracks) to the remote video/audio element so the local user can hear the other person. Ensure the audio element or video element used for remote playback has its srcObject set to the remote stream, is not muted, and that autoPlay is set. Also verify that getUserMedia requests audio: true so the local microphone track is captured and available for the simulated remote audio path.

quotes: I can't hear the other person's on the call

acceptanceCriteria: The VideoCallModal renders a dedicated audio or video element for the remote stream that has its srcObject set and is not muted.
getUserMedia in useVideoCall.ts requests both audio and video (audio: true, video: true).
The remote audio element has autoPlay set to true so playback begins without user interaction.
No console errors related to audio playback or media streams occur during the call lifecycle.
Ending the call stops all media tracks and removes the srcObject from the remote audio/video element.

constraints: Do not alter immutable frontend paths: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui.
Since true peer-to-peer WebRTC is not available, the fix must work within the existing simulated call architecture.
nonGoals: Implementing real peer-to-peer WebRTC audio/video transmission.
Changes to the backend Motoko actor.
Changes to any other feature outside of the video call audio fix.
